summary: Solid late‑1979 Denver show featuring a debut of Brent’s “Easy to Love You”, fiery “Samson & Delilah” and a monster “Playing in the Band” jam; audience recording is good for its era though slightly slow.
review: The performance shines with standout moments – the first outing of Brent Mydland’s “Easy to Love You”, an intense “Samson & Delilah”, tight “Ship of Fools”, and a spectacular “Playing in the Band” sequence that many reviewers call the night’s best jam. The audience tape captures the band well given 1979 technology, offering clear enough sound but requiring a modest speed‑up to correct pitch issues.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical set (especially second‑set jams) and rate the recording as superior for an audience source; a few note pitch/tempo quirks, pulling the overall score slightly down.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear analog capture for its time; praised as “superior audience recording” but noted to be a bit slow and needing ~+1 % pitch correction. No major drop‑outs reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as average/average‑warmup, while the second set delivers the high points – a tight “Promised Land”, an uplifting “Ship of Fools”, a powerful “Samson & Delilah” jam, and the show‑defining “Playing in the Band”. Overall pacing improves dramatically after the midway break.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for taking the horns on ‘Samson & Delilah’, delivering his best work on the final minutes of ‘Stella Blue’, and leading the jam on ‘Playing in the Band’.
band_member_comments.Keys: Mention of Brent Mydland’s brand‑new composition “Easy to Love You”, indicating keyboard contribution, but no specific playing critique.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart praised for an extended, “sidereal” Space section; drums transition noted as remarkable in the Terrapin > Playing sequence.
